achievements.category:
Computer knowledgeSoftwareI have been using Windows since 2001 so I study well. It all started with Windows 98. And computer maintenance was expensive at the time, so I wanted to learn it myself.And I did it!Now I can help you too.
        Linux knowledge is also extensive. I've mostly used Ubuntu family distributions, currently I'm using Kubuntu full-time, instead of Windows 11, which became really buggy when AI started programming them. But I have also tried other distributions, such as ArchLinux.'BTW i Used Arch!'I think everyone who likes computers knows this humor.HardwareFrom A to Z. From building computers to finding a problem and solving it.NetworkCreating a network and configuring the network and its devices. Finding problems in the network and solving them. I have used TP-Link, Asus devices, and devices with the OpenWRT system. I know how to use network diagnostic tools. I also had a Mikrotik router for a short time, so I have tried this equipment as well. I have not used Ubiquiti, but I have watched many YouTube videos. I am quite familiar with its configuration, and I think it is a very good and, most importantly, easy-to-manage system. I have not tried Teltonika products, but if you give me the opportunity, I would really like to try it.InternetVPSI know how to configure a VPS server from scratch and run the necessary apps. I know how to run and manage Docker containers, but I haven't tried Kubernetes at all.
        I know what TLS-SSL is and how to get them from the Let's Encrypt organization and how to install them on a website, devices, phones, and elsewhere.
        I know how to create an email service on a server. I also know how to create an email service with the desired domain - from purchasing a domain to fully launching it. And it doesn't matter what it is, an email service or a website. This is how I made myself an email with a domain that is hosted on "zoho.com" and connected to a contact form. At that time, it was a free service that also provided free IMAP and SMTP services, which I also use. This website also uses my domain, which is managed and protected by Cloudflare.DockerI use the Docker system. I manage containers through Dockhand, which is much more convenient than Portainer, which I used before. I can run a container. Make it work through a Cloudflare reverse proxy so that there is no need to open ports to the outside. Also create a separate network for containers and connect them both. For example, Tailscale with Plex. After deleting a container, mount the old container folder with settings to the new container. Constantly update them automatically. Dockhand solves that problem very easily. Receive notifications on your phone if something is wrong. And check containers for viruses with two antiviruses, even before installing and updating them. Because of this, unfortunately, I had to say goodbye to Ollama. When updating to the latest version, it found as many as 52 violations. One of them is critical, the others are smaller.CloudflareI know how to use Cloudflare. Cloudflare DNS services for this domain, and my other project domains. All protections are possible, both for this project and others, wherever possible and as possible. Reverse proxy, tunnel, etc.AWSI like to try new things for myself. That's why I participated in theAWS Cloud Quest: Cloud Practitionertraining. The course duration was about 12 hours, but with my knowledge I finished everything in 5 hours. It was very interesting how the famous AWS is configured. I learned that it is interesting and not very difficult. I think I will continue my education in this area. 
            I also have several other AWS certifications. One of them is visible in the 'About Me' section.CMSI know how to configure CMS systems, add payment methods, product lists, updates, plugins, etc. I can use CMS systems such as WordPress, WooCommerce, Shopify, Squarespace. I would easily learn to use others as needed. Although I don't have much programming knowledge, I know how to edit code, how to fix errors, connect TLS-SSL certificates, etc.Personal projectI have a Raspberry Pi 5 with 16GB RAM and 4TB NVMe. And I use it as a laboratory for practical work. Thanks to it, I learned very well how to manage Docker containers, and how to manage them with the GUIPortainernow Dockhand. And everything else related to them. Also Cloudflare tunnel, etc. Thanks to it, you can now see my CV here. Which is also run in a container. It also helped me a lot to expand my Linux knowledge. I use the default Raspberry Pi OS, which is based on Debian.This is my introduction website, made with the Python programming language and the Django framework, and the Bootstrap system was used for the design. And also to improve JavaScript performanceI know how to edit HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, JavaScript and Python code. Python has a very easy-to-understand syntax for me and it has many useful libraries, so it is very easy and fun to use. That's why I like it very much. In my free time, I learn the secrets of Django.I am also interested in internet marketing. I manage one Google Business Profile account.
        I have also completed a dropshipping course, "DS mokykla", the founder of which is Indrė Prichockaitė, and I have also completed a liquidation goods course there. I would like to try to buy a pallet with laptops and, after fixing them, giving the product a warranty, try to sell, but I don't have the premises for that.

achievements.details:
I have been using Windows since 2001 so I study well. It all started with Windows 98. And computer maintenance was expensive at the time, so I wanted to learn it myself.And I did it!Now I can help you too.
        Linux knowledge is also extensive. I've mostly used Ubuntu family distributions, currently I'm using Kubuntu full-time, instead of Windows 11, which became really buggy when AI started programming them. But I have also tried other distributions, such as ArchLinux.'BTW i Used Arch!'I think everyone who likes computers knows this humor.
From A to Z. From building computers to finding a problem and solving it.

Creating a network and configuring the network and its devices. Finding problems in the network and solving them. I have used TP-Link, Asus devices, and devices with the OpenWRT system. I know how to use network diagnostic tools. I also had a Mikrotik router for a short time, so I have tried this equipment as well. I have not used Ubiquiti, but I have watched many YouTube videos. I am quite familiar with its configuration, and I think it is a very good and, most importantly, easy-to-manage system. I have not tried Teltonika products, but if you give me the opportunity, I would really like to try it.

I know how to configure a VPS server from scratch and run the necessary apps. I know how to run and manage Docker containers, but I haven't tried Kubernetes at all.
        I know what TLS-SSL is and how to get them from the Let's Encrypt organization and how to install them on a website, devices, phones, and elsewhere.
        I know how to create an email service on a server. I also know how to create an email service with the desired domain - from purchasing a domain to fully launching it. And it doesn't matter what it is, an email service or a website. This is how I made myself an email with a domain that is hosted on "zoho.com" and connected to a contact form. At that time, it was a free service that also provided free IMAP and SMTP services, which I also use. This website also uses my domain, which is managed and protected by Cloudflare.

I know how to configure a VPS server from scratch and run the necessary apps. I know how to run and manage Docker containers, but I haven't tried Kubernetes at all.
        I know what TLS-SSL is and how to get them from the Let's Encrypt organization and how to install them on a website, devices, phones, and elsewhere.
        I know how to create an email service on a server. I also know how to create an email service with the desired domain - from purchasing a domain to fully launching it. And it doesn't matter what it is, an email service or a website. This is how I made myself an email with a domain that is hosted on "zoho.com" and connected to a contact form. At that time, it was a free service that also provided free IMAP and SMTP services, which I also use. This website also uses my domain, which is managed and protected by Cloudflare.

I use the Docker system. I manage containers through Dockhand, which is much more convenient than Portainer, which I used before. I can run a container. Make it work through a Cloudflare reverse proxy so that there is no need to open ports to the outside. Also create a separate network for containers and connect them both. For example, Tailscale with Plex. After deleting a container, mount the old container folder with settings to the new container. Constantly update them automatically. Dockhand solves that problem very easily. Receive notifications on your phone if something is wrong. And check containers for viruses with two antiviruses, even before installing and updating them. Because of this, unfortunately, I had to say goodbye to Ollama. When updating to the latest version, it found as many as 52 violations. One of them is critical, the others are smaller.

I know how to use Cloudflare. Cloudflare DNS services for this domain, and my other project domains. All protections are possible, both for this project and others, wherever possible and as possible. Reverse proxy, tunnel, etc.

I like to try new things for myself. That's why I participated in theAWS Cloud Quest: Cloud Practitionertraining. The course duration was about 12 hours, but with my knowledge I finished everything in 5 hours. It was very interesting how the famous AWS is configured. I learned that it is interesting and not very difficult. I think I will continue my education in this area. 
            I also have several other AWS certifications. One of them is visible in the 'About Me' section.

I know how to configure CMS systems, add payment methods, product lists, updates, plugins, etc. I can use CMS systems such as WordPress, WooCommerce, Shopify, Squarespace. I would easily learn to use others as needed. Although I don't have much programming knowledge, I know how to edit code, how to fix errors, connect TLS-SSL certificates, etc.

I have a Raspberry Pi 5 with 16GB RAM and 4TB NVMe. And I use it as a laboratory for practical work. Thanks to it, I learned very well how to manage Docker containers, and how to manage them with the GUIPortainernow Dockhand. And everything else related to them. Also Cloudflare tunnel, etc. Thanks to it, you can now see my CV here. Which is also run in a container. It also helped me a lot to expand my Linux knowledge. I use the default Raspberry Pi OS, which is based on Debian.This is my introduction website, made with the Python programming language and the Django framework, and the Bootstrap system was used for the design. And also to improve JavaScript performanceI know how to edit HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, JavaScript and Python code. Python has a very easy-to-understand syntax for me and it has many useful libraries, so it is very easy and fun to use. That's why I like it very much. In my free time, I learn the secrets of Django.I am also interested in internet marketing. I manage one Google Business Profile account.
        I have also completed a dropshipping course, "DS mokykla", the founder of which is Indrė Prichockaitė, and I have also completed a liquidation goods course there. I would like to try to buy a pallet with laptops and, after fixing them, giving the product a warranty, try to sell, but I don't have the premises for that.
